title: Revisiting the mental imagery debate: New evidence from aphantasia and neuroimaging.

assets.abstract: In this viewpoint letter, I critically examine the longstanding debate regarding the nature of mental imagery-specifically the tension between depictive and propositional theories-through the lens of recent neuroscientific findings. While early studies using neuroimaging were interpreted as supporting a depictive, perception-like model of visual imagery, emerging data from individuals with aphantasia present compelling counterevidence. These individuals, who report an absence of conscious visual
